summary:
Only the declared output changed (input.json was already modified before I started, per the initial git status). The step is complete.

**Summary:** I updated `open_questions.md` for iteration 2 of the Define phase gate. The requester had already confirmed the prior iteration's defaults ("Defaults look good"), so I kept those as a condensed "Confirmed" section rather than re-litigating them, and focused the new content on the human-in-the-loop answer modal (R36–R50) that requirements-writer added in response to the new feedback. Rather than speculating, I traced the actual codebase (`Phases::AnswerQuestions`, `_define_panel.html.erb`, `Phases::SendBack`/`Approve`) to find that a purpose-built, Define-phase-only, single-free-text-string answering mechanism already exists — the same "big free-text box" the requirements describe replacing. That grounding surfaced four real open questions the requester needs to weigh in on: whether to keep the service's existing (non-configurable) target-step logic, whether structured per-question answers get composed into one string for the existing service or require extending the feedback data model to carry per-question attribution, whether the modal stays Define-phase-only like the underlying service, and whether the existing inline free-text box gets removed once the modal ships.
